differential_diagnoses: Postpartum major depressive disorder with psychotic features (intrusive thoughts vs. delusions)
Adjustment disorder with anxiety and depressed mood
next_steps: Conduct a comprehensive psychiatric assessment to determine risk of harm and need for inpatient versus intensive outpatient care
Provide psychoeducation and reassurance regarding the nature of ego-dystonic intrusive thoughts in the postpartum period
Optimize pharmacotherapy, likely by increasing the dose of an SSRI and considering an augmentation strategy
Initiate specialized cognitive behavioral therapy focusing on exposure and response prevention for OCD symptoms
Ensure safety planning, including close monitoring and support by family members
Avoid unnecessary invasive medical testing unless clinical signs of organic etiology emerge
Prioritize maternal-infant attachment and support systems without immediate separation unless safety is compromised
